title: Agent shows raw 403 HTML response instead of a friendly error message

labels.name: triaged
labels.description: Issue has received an initial automated triage pass.

labels.name: repro:medium
labels.description: The report suggests a plausible repro path, but some uncertainty remains.

labels.name: area:agent
labels.description: Agent workflows, conversations, prompts, cloud mode, and AI-specific UI.

body:
### What happened?

When an agent request is rejected by the backend with a 403, Warp surfaces the raw HTML error page inline in the conversation. The error includes `<!doctype html>`, a `403 Forbidden` heading, and server-generated markup instead of a product-level explanation.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start an agent action that requires access to a resource the account cannot read.
2. Let the request fail with a 403.
3. Inspect the assistant message shown in Warp.

### Expected behavior

Warp should show a concise error such as:
`I do not have permission to access that resource. Check your account permissions or try signing in again.`

### Actual behavior

The raw HTML response is displayed directly in the agent transcript.

### Why this matters

The raw response is noisy, hard to understand, and makes the agent look broken even when the underlying problem is just authorization.
